title: alchemilla pubescens var. flabellata
intro: Alchemilla pubescens var. flabellata, also known as the Lady's Mantle, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its rounded, toothed leaves and drooping white flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla pubescens var. flabellata make it beneficial for treating health issues such as digestive problems, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations, with its health benefits including reducing bleeding, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Alchemilla pubescens var. flabellata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
